explanatory_strength.rationale: The ETH look-through discount mechanism is specific and filing-backed, but the proposed gap-closer is passive mNAV normalization over seven years rather than a controllable liquidation, sale, or distribution path that Klarman requires to convert appraisal value into shareholder cash.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: May 31, 2026 ETH fair value, share count, warrant liability, and deferred tax are primary-verified in the 10-Q, yet BMNP preferred claims, post-May ATM issuance, and live ETH spot versus May marks remain unresolved for a decision-grade realization audit.

downside_control.rationale: Static ETH fair value per share (~$18.76) sits above price today, but the component low case ($8.50/sh, -46% from $15.69) and unbounded ATM dilution mean per-share asset floor is not structurally bounded despite partial dhando.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base annualized return 6.3% over seven years is well below the 15% Klarman hurdle; only the bull case (~15.1%) clears it and depends on favorable ETH trajectory plus limited further dilution.

claims.claim: Filing-verified ETH look-through (~$18.76/sh at May 31, 2026) exceeds price ($15.69), but there is no independently evidenced catalyst to convert that asset into per-share cash beyond hoping the mNAV discount narrows.

The eth_trajectory_net_dilution component (+$4.5/sh base) lacks explicit success probability and post-May ATM reconciliation, so incremental asset value is analyst judgment rather than contractually or operationally secured.

Overlap controls in the component schedule are clean (unique keys, no double-count of static ETH with staking yield), but the warrants/tax/preferred reserve row at base zero understates senior claims relative to common look-through.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market may rationally price BMNR as an equity-financed ETH accumulator where ongoing ATM issuance will dilute look-through ETH per share faster than any mNAV discount can close, making the apparent NAV discount a financing-vehicle trap rather than a catalyst-backed margin of safety.
most_important_missing_fact: Quantified post-May 2026 ATM issuance pace and BMNP preferred-stock waterfall per diluted common share, without which per-share asset realization cannot be bounded.
falsifiers: ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises for two consecutive quarters.
Preferred or warrant conversion materially reduces common look-through below the component low case ($10.50/sh on static ETH look-through).
ETH fair value per share falls below the low case after sustained spot decline and further dilution.
Management announces a dated liquidation, buyback, or distribution program that converts treasury ETH into per-share cash on a defined timeline.
specialist_findings: Component audit: eth_look_through_today is filing-tier unit NAV and appropriately separated from trajectory; eth_trajectory_net_dilution is additive with null success_probability and no comparable realization precedents; staking_and_legacy_cash is immaterial and non-overlapping; warrants_tax_preferred_reserve base zero pending preferred review is the largest senior-claim gap. No comparable peer liquidation or forced-sale evidence is cited. Realization friction is high: ETH is marked fairly on balance sheet but common holders have no contractual path to cash except secondary sale or future corporate action. G&A ($335.7M nine months) exceeds staking revenue ($56.9M), eroding any operating bridge to asset realization. Price trades at ~0.84x May ETH fair value, offering a nominal discount, but Klarman would not treat this as cash-equivalent NAV without a dated catalyst and bounded dilution.
